commands: web search: conformal dark U(1) Coleman-Weinberg first order phase transition gravitational waves PTA NANOGrav
web search: dark U(1) Coleman-Weinberg phase transition gravitational waves NANOGrav PTA
web search: classically conformal U(1) dark sector gravitational waves phase transition
warnings: No paper found that exactly matches the supplied no-fermion, charge-one, m_A'=g_D v convention and also performs a PTArcade-ready PTA fit.
The closest minimal U(1)_D NANOGrav benchmark uses a different charge/mass convention, m_ZD = 2 g_D M.
Several directly relevant calculations use Landau-gauge one-loop finite-temperature potentials, so gauge dependence is a theory uncertainty.
Some PTA-scale conformal dark-sector papers assume dark/visible thermal decoupling, whereas the target prompt assumes xi = 1.
approximations: Benchmark extraction was preliminary and focused on published tables, captions, and convention sections rather than a full reproduction of each numerical scan.
INSPIRE-HEP was used as a metadata cross-check for key papers; detailed physics extraction used the arXiv PDFs converted to text.

papers.title: Gravitational waves from a dark U(1)_D phase transition in the light of NANOGrav 12.5 yr data
papers.brief_description: Closest prior study of a classically conformal dark U(1)_D phase transition giving a NANOGrav-scale stochastic GW signal.
papers.model_studied: Classically conformal U(1)_D extension with a complex scalar singlet, dark gauge boson, and optional vectorlike fermion/neutrino-sector additions; portal and Yukawa effects are neglected in the FOPT calculation.
papers.similarity_to_target: Same core dark Abelian Higgs/Coleman-Weinberg/FOPT/PTA structure, but uses a scalar charge and mass convention with m_ZD = 2 g_D M rather than the prompt's m_A' = g_D v.
experimental_constraints: Planck/BBN/CMB Delta N_eff pressure on light dark photons
Sub-GeV dark matter cosmology constraints when optional fermion is included

benchmark_points.label: NANOGrav benchmark
benchmark_points.notes: Table I/Fig. 1/Fig. 3 benchmark; paper states it is consistent with NANOGrav 12.5-year regions at 95 percent CL.

benchmark_points.label: scan range
benchmark_points.notes: Gauge coupling varied in this alpha_D range for alpha_*-T_* plots.

assumptions: Neglects portal coupling and dark-fermion Yukawa in the FOPT potential
Radiation domination with low-temperature g_* adjusted qualitatively

papers.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz gravitational waves from a classically conformal dark sector
papers.brief_description: Classically conformal hidden U(1)' model fitted to nano-Hz PTA data with PTArcade and sub-GeV dark matter constraints.
papers.model_studied: Hidden U(1)' with dark Higgs Phi, dark photon A', and two anomaly-cancelling left-handed fermions. Coleman-Weinberg breaking generates v and masses.
papers.similarity_to_target: Very close mass convention and target signal; differs by required fermions, Yukawa coupling, dark matter relic calculation, and kinetic mixing phenomenology.
experimental_constraints: Dark photon beam dump and NA64 constraints
BBN and CMB constraints from dark Higgs decays
assumptions: Landau gauge for one-loop finite-temperature potential
Dark and visible sectors thermally coupled before freeze-out in the studied scenarios
Sound-wave dominated GW signal with strong supercooling

papers.title: Gravitational waves and dark matter from classical scale invariance
papers.brief_description: Classically conformal U(1) dark-matter model with finite-temperature phase transitions and GW spectra, mostly at electroweak-scale frequencies.
papers.model_studied: Classically conformal U(1) model with scalar S, dark gauge boson, fermionic dark matter, Higgs portal, and Gildener-Weinberg symmetry breaking.
papers.similarity_to_target: Good conformal-U(1) structure match but not PTA scale and not the minimal no-fermion dark U(1) prompt.

papers.title: Gauge-independent gravitational waves from a minimal dark U(1) sector with viable dark matter candidates
papers.brief_description: Minimal dark U(1) dark-Higgs/dark-photon model with gauge-independent phase-transition and GW treatment in high- and low-temperature limits.
papers.model_studied: Minimal gauged U(1)_x dark sector with dark Higgs, dark photon, optional vectorlike dark fermion, Higgs portal or kinetic mixing.
papers.similarity_to_target: Close dark Abelian Higgs structure and very relevant gauge-dependence analysis, but not the exact classically scale-invariant Coleman-Weinberg target.
experimental_constraints: Kinetic mixing constraints from beam dumps, colliders, (g-2)_e and supernova
BBN lifetime requirements in benchmark dark-sector scenarios
assumptions: Gauge-independent Nielsen-identity treatment in controlled high- and low-temperature regimes
Intermediate T_p/m_Aprime region not controlled by those expansions
MS-bar Coleman-Weinberg terms in conventional gauge-dependent comparison

papers.title: NANOGrav Signal from a Dark Conformal Phase Transition
papers.brief_description: Nearly conformal dark confining phase transition producing a NANOGrav-scale GW signal.
papers.model_studied: Dark conformal sector with dark Yang-Mills dynamics, described via a dilaton/radion effective potential in a holographic picture.
papers.similarity_to_target: Same target signal and conformal motivation, but not a perturbative Abelian Coleman-Weinberg U(1) model.
experimental_constraints: Delta N_eff constraints for secluded dark radiation
assumptions: Dark and visible sectors initially thermally decoupled
Optimistic wall velocity v_w = 1 in examples

papers.title: Gravitational waves from first order electroweak phase transition in models with the U(1)_X gauge symmetry
papers.brief_description: Dark U(1)_X Abelian Higgs model with electroweak-scale multi-step phase transitions and GW signals.
papers.model_studied: SM plus dark U(1)_X gauge symmetry broken by a complex scalar S, with portal mixing and optional kinetic mixing or vector dark matter.
papers.similarity_to_target: Useful background for dark-Higgs/dark-photon conventions and constraints, but not classically conformal and not PTA scale.

benchmark_points.label: detectability summary
benchmark_points.notes: Not PTA-scale; included as background only.

novelty_verdict: Not novel as a broad class; partially novel only in the exact stripped-down convention and planned PTArcade implementation if no additional fermions are included.
novelty_notes: Dark U(1) Coleman-Weinberg/conformal first-order phase transitions have prior PTA-scale studies. The closest same-model paper is arXiv:2105.01007, but it uses a different dark charge/mass convention. arXiv:2502.19478 is a very close PTA/PTArcade conformal U(1)' study with matching m_A'=g v convention, but includes anomaly-cancelling fermions and dark matter dynamics absent from the target prompt.

conventions.vev_convention: hPhi = M/sqrt(2); prompt uses Phi=(chi+...)/sqrt(2) and v as the background vev
conventions.mass_convention: paper: m_ZD = 2 g_D M; prompt: m_A' = g_D v
conventions.fopt_definitions: alpha_* from released vacuum energy over radiation density at T_*; beta/H_* from S3/T derivative; T_* nucleation temperature

benchmark_hints.notes: Closest direct PTA-scale benchmark, but do not insert m_ZD directly without charge/vev translation.

conventions.mass_convention: m_A'^2(phi_b)=g^2 phi_b^2, matching prompt for charge one
conventions.renormalisation: MS-bar; Lambda identified with v for Coleman-Weinberg minimization

benchmark_hints.notes: Best guide for PTArcade priors and PTA-scale conformal U(1), but the target no-fermion model may shift thermal masses and viable g ranges.

conventions.gauge_treatment: gauge-independent Nielsen identity in controlled high-/low-temperature regimes
conventions.renormalisation: MS-bar for Coleman-Weinberg terms in conventional comparison

benchmark_hints.notes: Use mainly to budget gauge-dependence and to set broad scale expectations.
Useful for formalism but not for PTA priors.

conventions.model: confining nearly conformal dark sector, not Abelian Coleman-Weinberg

benchmark_hints.notes: Useful for PTA-scale conformal transition intuition and Delta N_eff caveats.

constraint_summary: MeV-scale dark photons and dark scalars are constrained by BBN, CMB, Delta N_eff, late decays, and laboratory dark-photon searches once kinetic mixing or other portals are specified.
The exact target prompt assumes small portal/kinetic mixing for FOPT dynamics, so collider and beam-dump constraints require a separate constraint-agent analysis after the model convention is fixed.
If the dark sector remains thermally coupled to the visible sector with xi=1, secluded-sector benchmark assumptions from some conformal papers are not directly transferable.
theory_uncertainties: Gauge dependence of the finite-temperature effective potential in Abelian gauge theories.
Choice of daisy resummation scheme and truncation order.
Supercooling/percolation failure and reliability of beta/H definitions in slow transitions.
Translation between different U(1) charge and vev conventions.
Thermal-mass changes if optional fermions are removed from structurally similar models.
recommended_next_steps: Before FOPT implementation, fix the U(1)_D charge normalization and verify that m_A'=g_D v is the intended physical mass.
Use arXiv:2105.01007 as the closest same-model seed after converting m_ZD=2 g_D M to the prompt convention.
Use arXiv:2502.19478 to set broad PTArcade/PTA priors around v=10 MeV-1 GeV and MeV-scale transition temperatures, while flagging fermion effects.
Track Landau-gauge and Arnold-Espinosa assumptions in downstream FOPT outputs.
Have the constraint agent check BBN, CMB/Delta N_eff, dark photon laboratory constraints, SN1987A, and late dark-scalar decay constraints once portal parameters are specified.
